programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A modeling agent convinces Robert to pose for head shots, but when he returns for the pictures, he finds that the agency has mysteriously disappeared.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert and Ray are shocked when they meet a man who considers Frank to be a mentor; Ray and Robert wonder how Frank could inspire anyone.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray discovers that Debra becomes more passionate when she argues with Marie, so he concocts a series of lies in an attempt to boost his love life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray's lie causes Debra to make a fashion statement to the members of the PTA.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Father and son nightclub performers are injured in Fred's house.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred's lifestyle is disrupted when Lamont runs for state assemblyman.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred, Grady and Bubba become contestants on a television game show, hoping to win a birthday present for Lamont.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred buys half ownership of a professional fighter who can't fight.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred awaits a visit from his sister until he finds out that her new husband is white.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: After a doctor fails to find the reason for his headache, Lamont consults a psychiatrist to treat his pain.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred asks a professional gambler to teach Lamont and his friends a lesson.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: When Lamont falls for an older woman, Fred schemes to show him how ridiculous it is.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Lamont and Rollo mistakenly think Fred has only six months to live and set him up on a date with Lena Horne.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Grady feels out of place when he moves in with his daughter and her family.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred puts his home and business up for sale when he hears an intense earthquake will hit and looks for a shelter in Las Vegas.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Esther fights with Woodrow and moves into the Sanford house. Fred comes up with plans to patch things up between them and send Esther home.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred is inside the bank when it gets robbed, and becomes a hostage.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont take in boarders to pay the mortgage, but the prospects don't look good.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont see a television series that resembles their lives. They become furious and prepare to sue the network.

programme.title: Sanford & Son
programme.sub-title: Brother, Can You Spare an Act
programme.desc: Fred goes into show business in order to get his brother-in-law out of the house.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Christine (Markie Post) receives and accepts an unexpected marriage proposal from Officer Tony Giuliano (Ray Abruzzo). With Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: A pregnant Christine (Markie Post) tries in vain to reach her new husband in South America. With John Larroquette.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Dan's new late-night television talk show raises eyebrows, especially those of guest Christine Sullivan.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Harry's (Harry Anderson) lousy day gets worse when he is forced to jail Mel Tormé for contempt. With Charlie Robinson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Old friend of the court Yakov (Yakov Smirnov) asks for his father's visa to be extended. Arte Johnson also guest stars. With Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Bull (Richard Moll) is persuaded to sue when he accidentally shoots himself in the foot with Roz's (Marsha Warfield) gun. Guest star: Gordon Clapp.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Harry, Bull and Dan (Harry Anderson, Richard Moll, John Larroquette) all pitch in to help at Christine's (Markie Post) Lamaze class.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.sub-title: Still Another Day in the Life
programme.desc: The court tries to conduct business while loud construction work takes place upstairs. With Harry Anderson and Markie Post.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Television viewers get an eyeful when the cameras of "A Closer Look" invade Harry's (Harry Anderson) courtroom. Bob Sarlatte guest stars. With Markie Post.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Christine (Markie Post) goes into labor while she's stuck in a courthouse elevator with Buddy and a stranger (John Astin, Stephen Furst).

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Dan's sister (Susan Diol), who is cut from the same cloth, drops by for a visit and a "fling." With Harry Anderson and John Larroquette.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Bull (Richard Moll) proposes to Dan's amorous sister (Susan Diol) after being accused of seducing her. With John Larroquette.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Harry's (Harry Anderson) first date with court reporter Margaret Turner (Mary Cadorette) is a flop when he follows Dan's (John Larroquette) advice.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Dan (John Larroquette) is thrilled to be the prize at a women's club auction -- until he sees the highest bidder. Marilyn McCoo guest stars.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: In a series of flashbacks, each night court member recalls a particular turning point. Stars Harry Anderson, John Larroquette, Markie Post.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: In a series of flashbacks, each night court member recalls a particular turning point. Stars Harry Anderson, John Larroquette, Markie Post.

programme.desc: The women pressure Ray and Frank to accompany Robert to one of his therapy sessions.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Dan (John Larroquette) sees his obituary in the paper after Harry (Harry Anderson) sends the "spirit of death" (Stephen Root) to jail.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: A temporary shift to the day session shows a different side of each staffer. With Marsha Warfield and Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.sub-title: A Night Court at the Opera
programme.desc: Dan (John Larroquette) uses a night at the opera to make his moves on Margaret (Mary Cadorette). With Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: A childhood bully (Don Gibb) confronts Harry (Harry Anderson) again after the judge puts him behind bars.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Christine (Markie Post) defends a young artist (Bill Calvert) and later falls victim to his charms. With Harry Anderson, John Larroquette.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: An offensive comic (Louis Mustillo) is hauled into Harry's court by a show-stopping conservative minister (Clifton James). With Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: An obnoxious child (Billy Cohen) who portrays a TV lawyer wreaks havoc while observing in Harry's (Harry Anderson) court. With Marsha Warfield.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: A man (Eric Christmas) who had his money in a failed bank makes a "withdrawal" from a bank officer's (John Ingle) home. With Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Harry (Harry Anderson) and the gang celebrate the return of Bull's seafaring mother (Paddi Edwards) with a whale of a party. With Richard Moll.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Bull (Richard Moll) visits a matchmaker (Cathy McAuley); Christine's (Markie Post) nerves are shot after she is served with divorce papers. With Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Harry (Harry Anderson) decides to propose to Margaret (Mary Cadorette) at Christine's (Markie Post) post-divorce party.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.sub-title: Hey Harry, F'Crying Out Loud -- It's a Wonderful Life ... Sorta
programme.desc: Harry's guardian angel (Mel Tormé) shows him how judicial life would be if he left the bench. With Harry Anderson, John Larroquette.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Dan (John Larroquette) suffers from insomnia caused by a guilty conscience; Bull (Richard Moll) wins cereal sweepstakes. Larry Linville guest stars.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.sub-title: With a Little Help From My Friends
programme.desc: Roz (Marsha Warfield) unwillingly joins Christine (Markie Post) at her singles support group and regrets every minute of it. With Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: Mac (Charlie Robinson) gets his college degree and a job offer from another judge; Dan (John Larroquette) hires a butler. Stars Harry Anderson.

programme.title: Night Court
programme.desc: A magazine votes Harry (Harry Anderson) one of New York's 10 most interesting men. With Markie Post and John Larroquette.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack agrees to pose in the nude for an art class.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: One of Janet's customers names her in his will.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack falls for a busy stewardess (Mary Cadorette).

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Janet and Phillip plan for the wedding at Janet's apartment; Jack wants to win the approval of Vicki's father; Mr Bradford tells Jack that Vicki was up for a promotion at work in another state and turned it down.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Vicky suggests that she and Jack live together after explaining her fear of marriage.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Chrissy and Janet try to convince their landlord that their new male roommate is gay.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother visits for the first time since Jack moved in with the girls.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: The roommates fear the rent money has been stolen.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack feels guilty about not standing up to a tough guy who makes a play for Chrissy.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Larry gives Jack and the girls a puppy even though Roper doesn't allow pets.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Convinced that Jack is gay, Roper decides that he is a safe date for his visiting niece.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack's modeling job requires him to pose nude.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack and Janet learn Chrissy's beau is married.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack throws a wild party when Chrissy and Janet go away for the weekend.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack and Chrissy walk in on Janet while she is entertaining a date; they attempt to set some rules so that they can be assured private time in the apartment with potential dates.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: The job of manager is available at Janet's flower shop; when she is passed over for a more buxom employee, she decides to change her appearance in order to compete.

programme.desc: Marie sees Frank putting salt on the lasagna, so she fears that she is losing her touch in the kitchen.
